codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses all 4 blocking issues from the M1 scrutiny validation. Each fix has been verified against the Python reference code and has appropriate test coverage. The implementation is clean and follows the project's porting conventions.

issues.description: Minor modulo bias in CreateContainerID: chars has 36 entries, and 256 % 36 = 8, so bytes 0-7 map to 'A'-'H' with probability 8/256 higher than other characters. Python's random.choice avoids this bias. The practical impact is negligible for container IDs (the bias is ~3% for 8 out of 36 chars), and the fix correctly solves the blocking issue of deterministic IDs. A perfectly unbiased implementation would use rejection sampling, but this is not worth blocking on.

EPUB2 mode is still missing the opf:event='publication' attribute on <dc:date> that Python sets (epub_output.py:906-907). This was not part of the fix scope and was already missing before — it's a pre-existing parity gap, not a regression. Python: pubdate.set(qname(ALT_OPF_NS_URI, 'event'), 'publication') for EPUB2.

EPUB2 mode is still missing the opf:scheme='uuid' attribute on <dc:identifier> when the UID starts with 'urn:uuid:'. Python does this at epub_output.py:872-873. This was noted in the original m1-epub-packaging review as a non-blocking issue and remains unfixed. Not a regression — outside the scope of this fix.

summary:
All 4 blocking issues from the M1 scrutiny validation are correctly fixed:

1. PERIODICAL_NCX_CLASSES: Changed from {0:'periodical', 1:'section', 2:'article'} to {0:'section', 1:'article'} — exactly matches Python epub_output.py:56-59. Test updated to verify correct classes and assert no 'periodical' class exists.

2. EPUB2 dc:creator opf:role: Added opf:role='aut' attribute on dc:creator for EPUB2 mode, matching Python epub_output.py:893-894. Also added xmlns:opf namespace declaration in the EPUB2 package element. Tests verify both the attribute and namespace.

3. Container format: Changed 'KFX_MAIN' to 'KFX main' in RebuildFragments, matching Python yj_container.py:15 CONTAINER_FORMAT_KFX_MAIN. Test data updated and new TestCreateContainerID_ContainerFormat test added.

4. CreateContainerID non-determinism: Replaced deterministic fastrandn() with crypto/rand. The fastrandn function was removed entirely. New TestCreateContainerID_NonDeterministic verifies different IDs on successive calls. Minor modulo bias exists (256%36=8) but is negligible for this use case.

All tests pass (epub: 12 NCX/OPF tests, kfx: 12 fragment validation tests + 2 new container ID tests). go vet is clean. No regressions introduced.
